pattern: Repeatedly folding a digit string into pairwise sums mod 10 is the same reduction Pascal's triangle performs on binomial rows -- just simulate it directly since the string is short.
hints: Try performing the described reduction by hand on a short example -- each pass shortens the string by exactly one digit.
Because the string is at most 100 characters, you can simulate every pass literally without worrying about performance.
Stop once exactly two digits remain, then just compare them.

edge_inputs.description: minimum length, three digits, folds once
all identical digits stay identical through every fold
carries wrap through 0 at several steps
four digits where the two results differ only after the final fold

stress.description: many random digit strings across the allowed length range
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(3, min(100, size if size >= 3 else 3))
    s = ''.join(str(rng.randint(0, 9)) for _ in range(n))
    return [s]
